function:
def priority(item: float, bins: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    """Returns priority with which we want to add item to each bin.

    Args:
        item: Size of item to be added to the bin.
        bins: Array of capacities for each bin.

    Return:
        Array of same size as bins with priority score of each bin.
    """
    ratios = bins / item
    priorities = ratios - np.log(ratios)
    return priorities
thought: The improved heuristic aims to prioritize bins that not only have sufficient remaining capacity but also maximize the utilization of space after placing the item, while also considering the distribution of remaining capacities among bins. To achieve this, the strategy combines the remaining capacity of each bin with a penalty for underutilization, using the formula \( \text{priority} = \frac{\text{bins}}{\text{item}} - \log\left(\frac{\text{bins}}{\text{item}}\right) \). This approach rewards bins with higher remaining capacity relative to the item size while penalizing those that would leave a large unused fraction, thereby promoting more balanced bin usage.
